methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Science Corporation is a Series B biotech & health company founded by Max Hodak that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 private same-stage companies in biotech & health (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent) alongside 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($200M) as a proxy base and applying a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $380M. Peer-median anchoring across the 5 private comparables yields $810M, with the peer set ranging from $600M (Profluent) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics). Public-comp anchoring is not applicable given the absence of disclosed revenue for the target. The peer median of $810M likely reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and clinical stage across the cohort.

The triangulated range across both methods is $323M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $595M. This midpoint suggests valuation approximately at the 25th percentile of the peer set, consistent with a company earlier in execution relative to leading biotech peers at the same stage. Key uncertainties include the target's clinical progress, IP position, and burn rate relative to peers—factors materially affecting risk-adjusted value but not visible in disclosed data.
